title: Enhance search results page and add voice search

requirements.text: Improve the SearchResultsPage to display richer search result cards: show video thumbnail, title, channel name, view count, upload date, and duration for each result. Add a results count indicator (e.g., 'X results for "query"') at the top of the results list.
acceptanceCriteria: Search results page shows each video's thumbnail, title, channel name, view count, upload date, and duration
A results count label is displayed above the results grid (e.g., '12 results for "cats"')
Loading skeleton states are preserved while results are being fetched
Empty state is shown when no results match the query

requirements.text: Add a voice search button to the search bar in the Layout navigation header. When clicked, it activates the browser's Web Speech API (SpeechRecognition) to capture spoken input, converts it to text, and automatically triggers a search by navigating to the search results page with the transcribed query.
acceptanceCriteria: A microphone icon button appears inside or adjacent to the search bar in the top navigation
Clicking the microphone button starts voice recognition using the browser's SpeechRecognition API
While listening, a visual indicator (e.g., animated mic icon or pulsing state) is shown
On speech recognition result, the transcribed text is placed in the search input and a search is triggered automatically
If the browser does not support SpeechRecognition, the microphone button is hidden or disabled gracefully
Errors during recognition (e.g., no speech detected) show a brief toast notification

constraints: Do not modify any files in frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
Voice search must use the native browser Web Speech API only — no external speech/AI service integrations
Backend changes are not required; search functionality already exists via useSearchVideos hook
nonGoals: Changes to the backend search logic or Motoko actor
